audit_items.proof_trace: All pointer dereferences in thr_nt.c operate on parameters that are always internal struct fields or stack addresses passed by libldap callers — never attacker-controlled null values. The external LDAP protocol cannot cause NULL to reach these internal threading API pointer arguments. Two related bugs exist (off-by-one guard `i > ntids` at L107 should be `i >= ntids`, and missing bounds check on `ntids < NT_MAX_THREADS` in thread_create), but neither produces a null pointer dereference — the static zero-initialized tids array causes WaitForSingleObject to receive a NULL HANDLE and return WAIT_FAILED gracefully, and the OOB write is a separate memory safety issue outside the NPD property scope.
